headline: BANK OF AMERICA HOLDS FIRM: $6,000 Gold Target Intact, Silver to Average $86 in 2026
snippet: Bank of America maintained its 12-month gold price target of $6,000/oz and forecast silver averaging $86/oz in 2026, citing persistent central bank buying and a weakening U.S. dollar as the primary drivers of the ongoing precious metals bull market.

headline: COT SNAPSHOT: Gold Speculator Longs Near Multi-Month High — May 1, 2026 Positioning Report
snippet: The latest Commitments of Traders data as of May 1 breaks down speculator positioning in gold, silver, and the U.S. dollar index, providing a weekly read on how large futures traders are positioned heading into the new trading week.

headline: CHINA SILVER SURGE: 836 Tonnes Imported in March Alone as Solar and Retail Investment Demand Collide
snippet: China's silver imports hit a record 836 tonnes in March 2026 as retail investment demand surged alongside industrial consumption from the solar sector, reinforcing projections for a sixth consecutive annual silver supply deficit of approximately 67 million ounces.

headline: RECORD $193 BILLION: Q1 Gold Demand Hits Value High as Bar-and-Coin Buying Surges 42%
snippet: Total Q1 2026 gold demand reached a record US$193 billion in value — up 74% year-on-year — as bar and coin demand surged to 474 tonnes (second-highest quarter on record) and central banks added 244 tonnes net, even as jewellery volumes fell 23% on record spot prices.

headline: NEW DEPTH: Radisson Hits 4.54 g/t Au over 12m at 1.9 Kilometres at O'Brien, Quebec
snippet: Radisson Mining extended high-grade gold at its Abitibi O'Brien project to a record 1,900 metres vertical depth, returning 4.54 g/t Au over 12.0m in the project's deepest-ever hole, with the 140,000-metre step-out program maintaining a 79% drill-hit rate across 97 holes.

headline: NEIGHBOUR PLAY: Visible Gold Raises C$2.6M to Drill Adjacent to IAMGOLD's 11-Million-Oz Nelligan
snippet: Visible Gold Mines closed a C$2.6 million financing to advance its Horsefly project in Quebec, positioned directly adjacent to IAMGOLD's 11-million-ounce Nelligan Mining Complex — one of Canada's largest undeveloped gold deposits.

headline: ACID CRUNCH: Middle East Conflict Cuts Sulphuric Acid Supply, Threatening 200,000t of Chilean Copper
snippet: Disrupted sulphur shipments from the Middle East have prompted China to restrict sulphuric acid exports — a critical input for roughly half of Chile's copper refining operations — putting up to 200,000 tonnes of annual output at risk as copper futures hold near $5.93/lb.

headline: LITHIUM REVIVAL: Battery Carbonate Nearly Doubles to $26,278/t in Q1 as Energy Storage Boom Restarts Idled Mines
snippet: Battery-grade lithium carbonate nearly doubled to US$26,278 per tonne in Q1 2026 as accelerating energy storage deployments tightened supply, prompting producers including Core Lithium to reverse care-and-maintenance decisions and restart idled Australian operations.

headline: CHINA TIGHTENS GRIP: New Rare Earth Enforcement Rules Impose Fines Up to 5x Illegal Gains
snippet: China's Ministry of Industry and Information Technology issued new enforcement rules this week imposing fines of up to five times illegal gains for violations across rare earth mining, separation, and processing, deepening state control over the global critical minerals supply chain.

headline: FOOTPRINT TRIPLES: Critical Elements Expands Rose West Lithium Discovery to 1,250m x 800m in Quebec
snippet: Critical Elements Lithium's Phase 1 winter drill program at the Rose West Discovery in Eeyou Istchee expanded the mineralized footprint nearly threefold — from 450m x 370m to 1,250m x 800m — with intercept thicknesses of 10 to 40 metres across a 10,000-metre program.

headline: AI POWER SURGE: Data Center Build-Out Pushes Uranium Spot Above $100/lb as Utility Contracting Lags
snippet: The global AI data center build-out is reshaping uranium demand forecasts for 2026, with spot prices back above $100/lb and prolonged utility under-contracting creating a potential supply crunch as new in-situ recovery projects ramp up across the U.S. and Canada.

headline: MINERS POISED TO OUTPACE METAL: Gold Equities Undervalued as Structural Bull Market Takes Hold
snippet: Analysts argue gold mining equities remain deeply undervalued relative to the metal's surge past $4,600/oz, with BMO Capital Markets describing the sector as entering a 'structural bull market' that should drive free cash flow yields well above historical norms.
